focus: D3 Traceability - Phase 007 auto-setup spec alignment against init script, lazy graph load, MCP startup logging, and setup guide coverage

focus: D2 Security - post-remediation recheck of compiler path boundaries, SQLite binding, and init shell execution
ruledOut: Direct ../ and absolute-path traversal are rejected by validate_derived_metadata() before filesystem existence checks.
Reviewed skill-graph-db.ts uses bound parameters for metadata reads/writes and node/edge mutation; no SQL injection surface found.
init-skill-graph.sh executes fixed quoted python3 command arrays without shell interpolation of untrusted input.
noveltyJustification: This pass materially changes the security trajectory by confirming the archived F010 traversal defect is closed in the current compiler and by ruling out adjacent SQL and shell injection concerns in the new graph-storage/setup surfaces.

focus: D3 Traceability - Phase 006 SQLite migration spec-to-code review for DB/query/handler/schema/dispatch surfaces
noveltyJustification: This pass confirmed the SQLite skill-graph DB/query/handler/schema/dispatch surfaces are present, then isolated two packet-level traceability defects: stale checklist evidence for scan output/registration and stale checklist semantics for hub_skills.
ruledOut: The four skill_graph_* tools are registered in tool-schemas.ts and the modular dispatcher; the drift is in the checklist wording, not missing code wiring.
All 10 query types named in the Phase 006 spec are implemented by the query handler and helper layer.

focus: D1 Correctness - post-remediation recheck of conflict penalty ordering, validator weight semantics, metadata weights, and SQLite loader parity
ruledOut: F040/F041-style validator regressions are not reproducible on the live corpus after remediation; validate-only emitted no weight-band or weight-parity warnings.
SQLite graph loading currently matches the compiled JSON artifact on schema_version, skill_count, families, adjacency, signals, conflicts, and hub_skills.
noveltyJustification: This pass materially changes the D1 trajectory by confirming that the archived F040/F041/F080/F081 defects are closed on the live code and metadata while narrowing F001 to a non-default pre-filter ordering nuance rather than a shipped default-path no-op.

focus: D3 Traceability - post-remediation recheck of gen1 blockers F020/F021/F022/F060 and hub-skills evidence
ruledOut: F020 no longer reproduces: spec/checklist now document schema versions 1 and 2 and the live skill-advisor metadata is on schema_version 2.
F060 no longer reproduces: handover compiler/graph references now use the skill-advisor/scripts layout.

focus: Gen2 final stabilization - rebuild findings registry, recheck former P0s, and issue verdict
noveltyJustification: No live routing or security regression survived the recheck. The remaining issues are packet-governance defects found by rereading the parent and phase docs after verifying that the former runtime blockers are closed.
ruledOut: Former runtime findings F001, F010, F040/F041, and F110 remain closed on the current code paths.
Former blocker themes F020/F021/F022/F060 no longer survive as active P0s after rereading the updated packet scope, checklist, and Phase 005 closeout evidence.

recoveryNote: Generation-2 state exposed prior work through JSONL runs 001-005 only, so this pass reconstructed the missing markdown lineage and remaining run from those records plus the archived gen1 packet and live rereads.
